apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of the Constitution to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of the Constitution on the lives of citizens.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of the Constitution in promoting social justice.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the historical context in which the Indian Constitution was framed.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the Indian Constitution with other democratic constitutions.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of the Constitution in promoting democracy and social justice.
remember: Students will be able to recall and list the key features of the Indian Constitution.
Students will be able to explain the basic structure of the Constitution.
Students will be able to identify the Preamble and its significance in the Constitution.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of a written Constitution and its importance.
Students will be able to explain the role of the Constitution in establishing a democratic government.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the Constitution and the rule of law.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: The students will be able to understand the concept of a written Constitution and its importance.
As per NCERT objective 2: The students will be able to explain the role of the Constitution in establishing a democratic government.
As per NCERT objective 3: The students will be able to analyze the relationship between the Constitution and the rule of law.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE will test the students' understanding of the key features of the Indian Constitution.
CBSE will test the students' ability to explain the role of the Constitution in establishing a democratic government.
CBSE will test the students' understanding of the relationship between the Constitution and the rule of law.
